response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong breadth across web, geospatial, and distributed systems, including a self-built GIS platform and DLang work on low-latency microservices handling millions of req/s. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ (or Rust) for routing tile pipelines, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with no recent C++ or Rust experience. Geospatial background and systems mindset are genuine pluses, but the core language requirement is a significant gap.
strengths: Real geospatial/GIS experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency distributed systems background at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testability, craftsmanship
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; last C/C++ role was ~2015
No direct navigation/routing or map tile pipeline work
